---
title: ""
page-layout: full
---

```{=html}
<div class="proj-page">

  <header class="proj-header">
    <div class="proj-header-inner">
      <div class="proj-back">
        <a href="../../index.html" class="proj-back-link">← All Projects</a>
      </div>
      <p class="proj-eyebrow">Exploration · Research &amp; Hands-On · Crypto AML</p>
      <h1 class="proj-title">Crypto &amp; Financial Crime</h1>
      <p class="proj-subtitle">
        An honest, in-progress exploration of on-chain analytics and the evolving
        regulatory landscape for virtual assets. Part research, part hands-on —
        tracing transaction flows on public blockchains, understanding how mixing
        and layering work in practice, and mapping where MAS and FATF guidance
        currently stands and where it doesn't.
      </p>
  </header>

  <!-- BODY -->
  <div class="proj-body">

    <!-- Section 1 -->
    <div class="proj-section">
      <div class="proj-section-marker">
        <span class="proj-section-num">I</span>
        <div class="proj-section-line"></div>
      </div>
      <div class="proj-section-content">
        <h2 class="proj-section-title">Why this, and why this framing</h2>
        <p class="proj-prose">
          Crypto compliance is the fastest-growing area in financial crime, and MAS is
          actively building its regulatory framework for digital payment token service
          providers under the Payment Services Act. But it is also genuinely complex —
          the technology moves faster than regulation, the terminology is contested, and
          a lot of what's written about it is either too technical or too superficial.
        </p>
        <p class="proj-prose">
          This is an exploration, not a finished project. The framing matters: I'd rather
          show the process of learning something rigorously than pretend to expertise I
          don't yet have.
        </p>
      </div>
    </div>

    <!-- Section 2 -->
    <div class="proj-section">
      <div class="proj-section-marker">
        <span class="proj-section-num">II</span>
        <div class="proj-section-line"></div>
      </div>
      <div class="proj-section-content">
        <h2 class="proj-section-title">Research thread</h2>
        <p class="proj-prose">
          The written component maps the current regulatory landscape: FATF's updated
          virtual asset guidance (2021, revised 2023), the travel rule and its
          implementation challenges across jurisdictions, MAS's Notice PSN02 and how
          Singapore's framework compares to the EU's MiCA. It also covers the core AML
          typologies specific to virtual assets — mixer and tumbler usage, chain-hopping,
          peer-to-peer networks, and DeFi as a layering vehicle.
        </p>
      </div>
    </div>

    <!-- Section 3 -->
    <div class="proj-section">
      <div class="proj-section-marker">
        <span class="proj-section-num">III</span>
        <div class="proj-section-line"></div>
      </div>
      <div class="proj-section-content">
        <h2 class="proj-section-title">Hands-on thread</h2>
        <p class="proj-prose">
          Using the Etherscan API and Elliptic's publicly released academic dataset
          (which contains labelled illicit and licit wallet addresses), the practical
          component traces transaction flows around known case studies — the Bitfinex
          hack of 2016 is fully documented on-chain and makes an instructive case study
          for how large-scale layering actually moves through a public ledger. The goal
          is a simple flow visualisation and hop analysis, not a predictive model.
          Seeing the data is the point.
        </p>

        <!-- Source rows -->
        <div class="proj-sources">
          <p class="proj-sources-label">Data Sources</p>
          <div class="proj-source-row">
            <div class="proj-source-index">01</div>
            <div class="proj-source-body">
              <p class="proj-source-name">Etherscan API</p>
              <p class="proj-source-desc">Public Ethereum transaction data — wallet balances, transfer histories, contract interactions.</p>
            </div>
          </div>
          <div class="proj-source-row">
            <div class="proj-source-index">02</div>
            <div class="proj-source-body">
              <p class="proj-source-name">Elliptic Academic Dataset</p>
              <p class="proj-source-desc">Labelled Bitcoin transaction graph with known illicit and licit classifications — used in academic research on on-chain anomaly detection.</p>
            </div>
          </div>
          <div class="proj-source-row">
            <div class="proj-source-index">03</div>
            <div class="proj-source-body">
              <p class="proj-source-name">MAS / FATF Guidance</p>
              <p class="proj-source-desc">Primary source regulatory documents — FATF virtual asset guidance, MAS PSN02, Payment Services Act — forming the research backbone.</p>
            </div>
          </div>
        </div>

      </div>
    </div>

    <!-- Living notice -->
    <div class="proj-notice">
      <span class="proj-notice-mark">✦</span>
      <p class="proj-notice-text">
        <em>This is a living exploration — research notes, code, and findings
        will be added here as the work develops.</em>
      </p>
    </div>

  </div>

</div>
```
